
# SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT AND SUPPORT AGREEMENT

**Contract Reference:** COHERON-SLA-[YEAR]-[SEQ]
**Version:** 1.0
**Related Agreement Reference:** [COHERON-CA-[YEAR]-[SEQ] / COHERON-SL-[YEAR]-[SEQ] / COHERON-MSA-[YEAR]-[SEQ]]
**Generated by:** CoheronConnect Legal Module · Coheron Tech Private Limited · Bengaluru, Karnataka, India
**Classification:** Confidential

---

## PARTIES

This Service Level Agreement and Support Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of **[EFFECTIVE_DATE]** ("Effective Date")

**BETWEEN:**

**1. [PROVIDER_LEGAL_NAME]**, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013, bearing CIN **[PROVIDER_CIN]**, GSTIN **[PROVIDER_GSTIN]**, having its registered office at **[PROVIDER_ADDRESS]**, India (hereinafter "Service Provider" or "Provider");

**AND**

**2. [CLIENT_LEGAL_NAME]**, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, [CLIENT_INCORPORATION_YEAR], bearing CIN **[CLIENT_CIN]**, having its registered office at **[CLIENT_ADDRESS]**, India (hereinafter "Customer").

---

## RECITALS

(A) Provider provides technology services, software, and/or infrastructure to Customer pursuant to a separate commercial agreement (the "Master Agreement") referenced above.

(B) The Parties wish to define the service levels, uptime commitments, support response targets, escalation procedures, and remedies applicable to the Services.

(C) This Agreement supplements and is incorporated into the Master Agreement. In the event of conflict between this Agreement and the Master Agreement on SLA matters, this Agreement prevails.

**NOW THEREFORE**, the Parties agree as follows:

---

## 1. DEFINITIONS

**1.1 "Availability"** means the percentage of time during a Measurement Period that the Service is operational and accessible by Customer, calculated as: **((Total Minutes in Period − Downtime Minutes) / Total Minutes in Period) × 100**.

**1.2 "Business Hours"** means 09:00 to 18:00 IST, Monday through Friday, excluding Indian public holidays ("Business Days").

**1.3 "Downtime"** means any period during which the Service is wholly unavailable to Customer due to Provider's failure, excluding Excluded Events.

**1.4 "Emergency Maintenance"** means unplanned maintenance required to prevent imminent harm to Service integrity, security, or data, commenced on short notice.

**1.5 "Excluded Events"** means: (a) Scheduled Maintenance; (b) Force Majeure Events; (c) outages caused by Customer's actions, misconfigurations, or third-party services outside Provider's control; (d) periods of Service suspension pursuant to the Master Agreement; and (e) Beta or trial features.

**1.6 "Incident"** means any unplanned interruption, degradation, or impairment to the Service.

**1.7 "Measurement Period"** means each calendar month during the Term of this Agreement.

**1.8 "Monthly Uptime Percentage" or "MUP"** means the Availability of the Service in a given Measurement Period, expressed as a percentage.

**1.9 "Priority Level"** means the severity classification assigned to a Support Ticket (P1, P2, P3, or P4) as defined in Clause 5.

**1.10 "Resolution Time"** means the elapsed time from the moment Provider acknowledges a Support Ticket to the moment Provider notifies Customer that the Incident is fully resolved or a workaround is in place.

**1.11 "Response Time"** means the elapsed time from the moment Provider receives a Support Ticket to the moment Provider sends Customer its first substantive response acknowledging the Incident and beginning investigation.

**1.12 "Scheduled Maintenance"** means planned maintenance communicated to Customer in advance per Clause 3.5.

**1.13 "Service"** means the software, platform, or managed service described in Schedule 1 and governed by the Master Agreement.

**1.14 "Service Credit"** means a credit against future invoices issued by Provider to Customer as a remedy for failure to meet service level commitments, as specified in Schedule 2.

**1.15 "Support Ticket"** means a formal request for support submitted by Customer via the designated support channels.

**1.16 "Personal Data"** has the meaning assigned under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.

**1.17 "Dispute"** means any dispute, difference, controversy, or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement.

---

## 2. AVAILABILITY COMMITMENT (SLA-1)

**2.1 Monthly Uptime Target.** Provider commits to achieving a Monthly Uptime Percentage of not less than **ninety-nine point nine percent (99.9%)** for the Service in each Measurement Period ("Uptime Commitment").

**2.2 Availability Measurement.** Provider shall measure Availability using its internal monitoring systems. Monthly availability reports shall be: (a) compiled by Provider within five (5) business days following the end of each Measurement Period; (b) delivered to Customer's designated technical contact by email; and (c) made available via the Customer portal at **[PORTAL_URL]** or support system.

**2.3 Independent Measurement.** Where Customer disputes an availability calculation, the Parties shall first attempt to resolve the dispute through good faith review of both Parties' monitoring data. If the Parties cannot agree within fifteen (15) business days, either Party may appoint an independent expert (mutually agreed, or if not agreed within five (5) days, appointed by the Chairperson of the IT Committee of the Confederation of Indian Industry, Bengaluru) to determine the actual Availability for the disputed period. The expert's determination shall be final and binding. Costs of the expert shall be borne by the Party whose position deviated more from the expert's determination.

**2.4 Excluded Downtime.** Excluded Events shall not count as Downtime for purposes of calculating MUP.

**2.5 Degraded Service.** Where Provider becomes aware of material Service degradation (not total outage) that materially impairs Customer's ability to use the Service, Provider shall treat the affected period as Partial Downtime, weighted at fifty percent (50%) for Availability calculation purposes.

---

## 3. SCHEDULED MAINTENANCE

**3.1** Provider shall perform Scheduled Maintenance during the designated maintenance window: **[MAINTENANCE_WINDOW — e.g., Sunday 02:00 – 06:00 IST]** ("Maintenance Window").

**3.2** Provider shall notify Customer of Scheduled Maintenance: (a) at least **seventy-two (72) hours** in advance for routine maintenance; and (b) at least **seven (7) days** in advance for major maintenance or upgrades expected to cause more than thirty (30) minutes of service impact.

**3.3** Provider shall minimise the duration and impact of Scheduled Maintenance and shall provide Customer with: (a) a summary of the work to be performed; (b) the anticipated start time and duration; and (c) any expected impact on Service functionality.

**3.4** Emergency Maintenance may be performed with shorter notice or no advance notice in critical circumstances. Provider shall notify Customer as soon as practicable when Emergency Maintenance commences and shall provide a post-incident report within forty-eight (48) hours of completion.

**3.5** Scheduled Maintenance within the agreed Maintenance Window does not constitute Downtime for Availability calculation purposes.

---

## 4. PROACTIVE NOTIFICATION (SLA-2)

**4.1 P1 and P2 Outbound Alert.** Regardless of whether Customer has raised an inbound Support Ticket, Provider shall proactively notify Customer's designated contacts within **fifteen (15) minutes** of Provider becoming aware of any Incident classified as Priority 1 (Critical) or Priority 2 (High) affecting Customer's Service.

**4.2 Notification Channels.** Proactive P1/P2 notifications shall be sent simultaneously via: (a) telephone call to the Customer Emergency Contact specified in Schedule 3; and (b) email to the Customer Technical Contact and the Customer Management Contact in Schedule 3.

**4.3 Incident Updates.** Following the initial P1/P2 notification, Provider shall provide Customer with status updates: (a) every **thirty (30) minutes** for P1 Incidents until resolved; and (b) every **two (2) hours** for P2 Incidents until resolved.

**4.4 Incident Report.** Provider shall deliver a post-incident root cause analysis report to Customer within: (a) forty-eight (48) hours for P1 Incidents; and (b) five (5) business days for P2 Incidents. The report shall include: (i) incident timeline; (ii) root cause; (iii) impact summary; (iv) remediation steps taken; and (v) measures implemented to prevent recurrence.

**4.5 P3/P4 Communication.** Provider shall provide status updates for P3 and P4 Incidents as reasonably requested by Customer and at each material development in resolution.

---

## 5. SUPPORT TIERS AND RESPONSE TARGETS

**5.1 Priority Level Definitions.**

| Priority | Severity | Definition | Examples |
|---|---|---|---|
| **P1 — Critical** | Service Outage | The Service is completely unavailable or a core feature is non-functional for all or a majority of Authorised Users, with no workaround | Total platform outage; login failure for all users; data loss event |
| **P2 — High** | Major Degradation | A significant feature is impaired or unavailable, or performance is severely degraded, with no adequate workaround | Key workflow unavailable; severe latency; partial data unavailability |
| **P3 — Medium** | Moderate Impact | A feature is impaired or behaving unexpectedly, but a workaround is available or the impact is limited | Non-critical feature broken; intermittent errors; individual user access issue |
| **P4 — Low** | Minor / Query | Minor issue, cosmetic defect, or general query with no material operational impact | UI display issue; documentation question; feature request; billing query |

**5.2 Response and Resolution Targets.**

| Priority | Response Time | Resolution Time | Support Hours |
|---|---|---|---|
| **P1 — Critical** | **15 minutes** | **4 hours** | 24×7×365 |
| **P2 — High** | **1 hour** | **8 Business Hours** | 24×7×365 |
| **P3 — Medium** | **4 Business Hours** | **3 Business Days** | Business Hours |
| **P4 — Low** | **1 Business Day** | **5 Business Days** | Business Hours |

> Response and Resolution targets are measured from the time a Support Ticket is logged via an approved channel (excluding periods where Provider is awaiting information from Customer).

**5.3 Support Channels.**

| Channel | P1/P2 | P3/P4 |
|---|---|---|
| **Emergency Phone** | [SUPPORT_EMERGENCY_PHONE] | — |
| **Support Email** | [SUPPORT_EMAIL] | [SUPPORT_EMAIL] |
| **Support Portal** | [SUPPORT_URL] | [SUPPORT_URL] |
| **Phone (Business Hours)** | [SUPPORT_PHONE] | [SUPPORT_PHONE] |

**5.4 Ticket Logging.** Customer shall raise all Support Tickets through the approved channels. Each ticket should include: (a) clear description of the issue; (b) steps to reproduce; (c) impact on business operations; (d) Customer's proposed priority level (Provider may reclassify); and (e) relevant logs or screenshots.

**5.5 Priority Classification.** Provider shall classify each Support Ticket within thirty (30) minutes of receipt for P1/P2 issues, and within four (4) business hours for P3/P4. If Customer disagrees with Provider's classification, Customer may escalate per Clause 6.

---

## 6. ESCALATION MATRIX

**6.1** If a Support Ticket is not resolved within the Response or Resolution Time targets, Customer may escalate using the following matrix:

| Escalation Level | Trigger | Provider Contact | Customer Contact |
|---|---|---|---|
| **L1 — Support Lead** | Response Time missed | [PROVIDER_SUPPORT_LEAD] | [CLIENT_TECHNICAL_CONTACT] |
| **L2 — Technical Manager** | Resolution Time missed | [PROVIDER_TECHNICAL_MANAGER] | [CLIENT_IT_MANAGER] |
| **L3 — Director / VP** | L2 unresolved within 2 hours (P1) / 1 day (P2) | [PROVIDER_DIRECTOR] | [CLIENT_DIRECTOR] |
| **L4 — C-Suite** | L3 unresolved within 4 hours (P1) / 2 days (P2) | [PROVIDER_CSUITE] | [CLIENT_CSUITE] |

**6.2** Escalation contacts are set out in Schedule 3. Each Party shall keep Schedule 3 current and notify the other Party of any changes within two (2) business days.

---

## 7. SERVICE CREDITS

**7.1 Credit Entitlement.** If Provider fails to achieve the Uptime Commitment in a Measurement Period, Customer shall be entitled to a Service Credit against the next invoice, calculated as follows:

| Monthly Uptime Percentage | Service Credit |
|---|---|
| 99.0% – 99.89% | 10% of Monthly Fee for that period |
| 95.0% – 98.99% | 25% of Monthly Fee for that period |
| 90.0% – 94.99% | 50% of Monthly Fee for that period |
| Below 90.0% | 100% of Monthly Fee for that period |

**7.2 Credit Claim Process.** To receive a Service Credit, Customer must: (a) submit a credit claim in writing to [SUPPORT_EMAIL] within **fifteen (15) business days** of the end of the relevant Measurement Period; (b) include the Support Ticket reference numbers and a description of the Downtime experienced; and (c) confirm that the Downtime was not caused by an Excluded Event.

**7.3 Credit Limit.** Service Credits shall not exceed one hundred percent (100%) of the Monthly Fee for the relevant Measurement Period. Service Credits are Customer's sole and exclusive remedy for Provider's failure to meet the Uptime Commitment, except in cases of gross negligence or wilful misconduct.

**7.4 Credit Application.** Service Credits shall be applied against Customer's next invoice. Service Credits have no cash value and cannot be transferred, assigned, or redeemed for cash.

**7.5 Persistent Failure.** If Provider fails to meet the Uptime Commitment for three (3) or more consecutive Measurement Periods, Customer may terminate this Agreement (and the Master Agreement) for cause by giving thirty (30) days' written notice, without further liability except for outstanding payment obligations for Services received.

---

## 8. AUDIT RIGHTS (M-6)

**8.1 Records.** Provider shall maintain complete and accurate records relating to Service delivery, uptime measurements, and support performance for at least seven (7) years from creation or as required by law.

**8.2 Right to Audit.** On not less than fifteen (15) business days' written notice, and no more than once per calendar year (unless a prior audit revealed material discrepancy), Customer may audit Provider's records relating to: (a) Availability and uptime calculations; (b) support ticket logs and response times; and (c) DPDP Act compliance.

**8.3 Audit Conduct.** Audits shall be conducted during business hours, by Customer or a mutually agreed independent auditor bound by confidentiality, without unreasonable disruption, at Customer's expense — unless the audit reveals material non-compliance, in which case Provider bears reasonable audit costs.

---

## 9. DATA PROTECTION — DPDP ACT 2023 (M-3)

**Data Protection and Privacy — DPDP Act 2023.**

- (a) **Applicability.** Applies to the extent either party processes Personal Data in connection with this Agreement or the Master Agreement.
- (b) **Compliance.** Each party shall comply with all applicable provisions of the DPDP Act, 2023 and rules thereunder, as amended.
- (c) **Data Processor Obligations.** Where Provider processes Personal Data in delivering support or maintenance Services, Provider shall: (i) process only per Customer's documented lawful instructions; (ii) implement appropriate security measures per Section 8 of the DPDP Act; (iii) not engage sub-processors without Customer's written authorisation; (iv) notify Customer within seventy-two (72) hours of any Personal Data Breach; (v) assist with Data Principal rights; (vi) delete or return Personal Data on termination; and (vii) permit compliance audits.
- (d) **Consent.** Where processing requires consent, Customer as Data Fiduciary shall obtain valid consent from Data Principals.
- (e) **Cross-Border Transfers.** Personal Data shall not be transferred outside India except as permitted under Section 16 of the DPDP Act.
- (f) **Survival.** Obligations survive termination for as long as either party retains Personal Data.

---

## 10. REPORTING AND REVIEW

**10.1 Monthly Report.** Provider shall deliver to Customer a monthly Service performance report within five (5) business days of month-end, covering: (a) Monthly Uptime Percentage; (b) Scheduled Maintenance performed; (c) Incidents by priority; (d) Support Tickets opened, resolved, and pending; (e) Average Response Time and Resolution Time by priority; and (f) Service Credits accrued (if any).

**10.2 Quarterly Business Review.** The Parties shall hold a Quarterly Business Review ("QBR") within fifteen (15) business days of each quarter-end to review: (a) service performance trends; (b) open issues and improvement plans; (c) planned infrastructure or capacity changes; and (d) roadmap and service evolution.

**10.3 Annual SLA Review.** The Parties shall review and, if agreed, revise the service level targets in Schedule 2 annually, to reflect changes in technology, Customer requirements, and industry benchmarks.

---

## 11. FORCE MAJEURE (M-5)

**11.1** Neither party shall be liable for delay or failure to perform (other than payment obligations) caused by a Force Majeure Event: acts of God, natural disasters, fire, government-declared pandemic, war, terrorism, civil unrest, acts of government, nationwide strikes, third-party internet backbone or telecom failures, or court orders.

**11.2 Exclusions.** Economic downturns, revenue loss, inability to pay, foreseeable infrastructure failures within Provider's reasonable control, or staffing shortages are NOT Force Majeure Events.

**11.3 Notice.** Affected Party notifies in writing within five (5) business days, mitigates, and provides regular updates.

**11.4 Extended Events.** If the event continues for sixty (60) consecutive days, either party may terminate on thirty (30) days' written notice without liability, except for accrued payment obligations.

---

## 12. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY (M-8)

**(a)** EXCEPT AS IN CLAUSE 12(c), NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR FOR LOSS OF PROFITS, REVENUE, GOODWILL, DATA, OR BUSINESS OPPORTUNITY, HOWEVER ARISING.

**(b)** EXCEPT AS IN CLAUSE 12(c), PROVIDER'S TOTAL AGGREGATE LIABILITY FOR FAILURE TO MEET SERVICE LEVELS SHALL NOT EXCEED: (i) FEES PAID IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM WHERE THE AGREEMENT HAS BEEN IN FORCE TWELVE (12) OR MORE MONTHS; OR (ii) TOTAL FEES PAID FROM THE EFFECTIVE DATE WHERE THE AGREEMENT HAS BEEN IN FORCE LESS THAN TWELVE (12) MONTHS.

**(c)** These limitations do NOT apply to: (i) death or personal injury from negligence; (ii) fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation; (iii) DPDP Act obligations including Personal Data Breach liability; (iv) wilful misconduct or gross negligence; or (v) liability that cannot be excluded under Indian law.

**(d)** Service Credits are Customer's sole remedy for SLA breaches, except in cases of persistent failure under Clause 7.5 or the exclusions in Clause 12(c).

**(e)** Each party shall take all reasonable steps to mitigate any loss.

---

## 13. ELECTRONIC EXECUTION AND DIGITAL SIGNATURES (M-2)

Electronic signatures are valid, binding, and enforceable under the Information Technology Act, 2000 (as amended 2008). A digitally signed copy transmitted electronically is treated as an original. Neither party shall contest validity solely on grounds of electronic execution. Digital signatures shall comply with Schedule II of the IT Act, 2000.

---

## 14. GOVERNING LAW AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION (M-1)

**(a) Negotiation.** Disputes first referred to senior representatives for good faith resolution within thirty (30) days of written notice.

**(b) Arbitration.** If unresolved, finally resolved by binding arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996 (as amended 2021). Sole arbitrator mutually appointed within fifteen (15) days, failing which appointed by the High Court of Karnataka. Seat and venue: Bengaluru, Karnataka, India. Language: English. Award: final and binding.

**(c) Interim Relief.** Either party may seek interim or injunctive relief from courts in Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.

**(d) Exclusive Jurisdiction.** Courts at Bengaluru, Karnataka, India for non-arbitration matters.

**(e) Costs.** Each party bears its own costs unless otherwise ordered.

---

## 15. TERM AND TERMINATION

**15.1** This Agreement is co-terminus with the Master Agreement unless terminated earlier in accordance with this clause.

**15.2** Either Party may terminate this Agreement on sixty (60) days' written notice for any reason, provided the Master Agreement is simultaneously terminated or otherwise amended to remove the SLA reference.

**15.3** Either Party may terminate immediately on written notice if the other Party commits a material breach unremedied within thirty (30) days of written notice.

**15.4** Persistent SLA failure termination rights are set out in Clause 7.5.

---

## 16. GENERAL PROVISIONS

**16.1 Entire Agreement.** This Agreement and Schedules constitute the complete SLA and support terms between the Parties and supersede all prior representations on service levels.

**16.2 Amendments.** No amendment unless in writing signed by authorised representatives of both Parties.

**16.3 Notices.** All notices in writing by email with read receipt or registered post to the details in Schedule 3.

**16.4 No Waiver.** Failure to exercise any right is not a waiver.

**16.5 Severability.** If any provision is invalid, remaining provisions continue.
